title: Gateway API Role-Oriented Design
description:
## Gateway API Role-Oriented Design

Kubernetes Gateway API is designed to be role-oriented, splitting configuration across different API resources to match organizational roles.

Which of the following resources is typically managed by the **Infrastructure Provider** or **Cluster Operator** to define the template and controller for gateways, rather than by individual Application Developers?
explanation: `GatewayClass` is cluster-scoped and managed by the Infrastructure Provider/Cluster Operator. It defines a template of configurations (like the controller implementation: Envoy, Traefik, Istio, etc.) that can be instantiated. `Gateway` is usually managed by the Cluster Operator to define entrypoints, while `HTTPRoute` is managed by Application Developers to route traffic to their applications.

hints.title: Role division in Gateway API
hints.body: GatewayClass defines the template/implementation. Gateway defines the instance/entrypoint. HTTPRoute defines routing rules.
